Use Case Guide

Construction Software for Insurance Restoration Contractors

Restoration has a unique workflow — insurance claims, adjusters, supplements, and mitigation. Here's what actually works for the business.

✓ Updated April 2026

Insurance restoration is a distinct sector of the construction industry with workflows that don't fit general-purpose construction software. You're not selling to a homeowner with a budget — you're negotiating with an insurance adjuster over Xactimate scope. Your job doesn't begin with a signed contract; it begins with a claim number and an adjuster inspection. Understanding this distinction is essential for choosing the right tools.

What insurance restoration contractors need from software

  • Claim tracking: Pipeline of claims from initial contact through inspection, estimate approval, production, and settlement
  • Xactimate integration or compatibility: The industry-standard estimating format for insurance work
  • Supplement management: Track supplement requests, adjuster responses, and approved additional scope
  • Photo documentation: Organized before/after documentation linked to specific damage items for adjuster review
  • Mitigation coordination: If you also do mitigation, coordinate the emergency services phase with the rebuild phase
  • Assignment of benefits tracking: Know the legal status of each claim's payment flow
  • Customer communication: Homeowners in a restoration project are anxious and need proactive communication

The Xactimate reality

Xactimate (owned by Verisk) is the estimating software used by insurance carriers to calculate claim values. To negotiate effectively with adjusters, restoration contractors need to understand Xactimate pricing, line items, and scope conventions. Most restoration estimates start as Xactimate files provided by the adjuster. Your job is to review the scope, identify items that were missed or under-scoped, and supplement the estimate.

This means your project management software needs to either integrate with Xactimate or accommodate Xactimate estimates as a baseline. General construction PM tools that don't speak Xactimate create a translation problem.

Best software picks for insurance restoration

Purpose-built restoration CRM: Jobnimbus or AccuLynx (restoration module)

JobNimbus has a strong restoration-focused workflow — claim pipeline tracking, contact management for adjusters and homeowners, and photo documentation organized by job. For roofers doing storm damage insurance work, it's often the tool of choice. AccuLynx similarly handles the insurance roofing workflow with supplement tracking built in.

For larger restoration companies: Restoration Manager or RMS

Companies doing significant volume across water, fire, and storm damage restoration often use purpose-built platforms like Restoration Manager or similar restoration-specific ERPs. These platforms handle the full claim lifecycle from emergency call through final billing and include Xactimate integration, moisture mapping, equipment tracking (dehumidifiers, air movers), and carrier billing.

For general contractor restoration: Buildertrend

Restoration GCs who handle larger loss projects — major fire damage, significant water damage requiring full interior rebuild — find that Buildertrend handles the rebuild phase well even if it doesn't integrate with Xactimate for the initial claim. The project management workflow (scheduling, subcontractor coordination, change orders, client portal) is appropriate for the rebuild phase that follows the insurance negotiation.

Buildertrend — for the rebuild phase of restoration projects. Manage subs, schedules, and client communication through project completion.

See Buildertrend →

Photo documentation: your most important protection

In insurance restoration, photos are your evidence, your negotiation tool, and your protection against later disputes. Best practices:

  • Document everything at initial assessment: Every room, every surface, every piece of damaged equipment — before any mitigation work begins
  • Photo per line item: Adjuster line items without corresponding photos are more likely to be disputed. Match photos to scope items
  • Timestamp and GPS tag: Photos should include date/time and location metadata — both Raken and purpose-built restoration tools do this automatically
  • Organized by area: Photos should be navigable by a third party (adjuster, appraiser, attorney) without your narration
  • Progress photos: Document tear-out conditions that won't be visible in finished photos

Supplement management workflow

Supplementing is the process of requesting additional scope coverage from the insurance carrier when the initial estimate is incomplete. An organized supplement process:

  1. Review adjuster's Xactimate estimate against actual site conditions
  2. Identify missed items (often: O&P, code upgrades, depreciation recovery, scope gaps)
  3. Prepare a supplement request with supporting documentation (photos, line item justification)
  4. Submit to the adjuster and track response
  5. Negotiate on disputed items
  6. Document approved supplement additions to the claim

Contractors who have a systematic supplement process recover significantly more from each claim than those who accept the initial estimate without review.

Customer communication in restoration

Restoration customers are often in a stressful situation — they've just had a fire or flood and are displaced or living in a damaged home. Proactive communication reduces calls, builds trust, and reduces disputes. Tools that auto-send status updates at project milestones (estimate approved, crew scheduled, work complete) create a communication cadence without constant manual effort.

Bottom line

Purpose-built restoration CRMs (JobNimbus, AccuLynx, Restoration Manager) are the right fit for contractors doing high-volume insurance work where the claim pipeline and supplement tracking are central to the business. For contractors doing occasional restoration work alongside general remodeling, Buildertrend can handle the rebuild phase management while you manage the insurance negotiation through your own process or a specialized tool.

Buildertrend manages the rebuild phase — scheduling subs, tracking costs, and communicating with homeowners through project completion.
